rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly in the theory setup, not just in a literature list. The citing paper explicitly adopts the Jain and Srivastava view that price-matching guarantees can signal retailer positioning to uninformed consumers, and it cites that work when motivating key assumptions about informed versus uninformed consumers. It then develops a new model and evidence around vertical service differentiation using that signaling logic, indicating a direct build-on role.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Jain and Srivastava (2000)'s signaling account of price-matching policies and its informed/uninformed consumer logic. It extends that line by introducing vertical service differentiation and deriving/tested predictions about which retailers adopt guarantees.
